summary: Matched two sysdolphin baselib shadow routines, HSD_ShadowRemove and HSD_ShadowInit, by replacing their hand-written assembly in asm/sysdolphin/baselib/shadow.s with C implementations in src/sysdolphin/baselib/shadow.c. The C version reconstructs shadow teardown logic, camera refcount handling, texture/image cleanup, and texture-copy initialization using GXSetTexCopySrc and GXSetTexCopyDst.
what_changed: Removed the assembly bodies for HSD_ShadowRemove and HSD_ShadowInit from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/shadow.s.
Added a C implementation of HSD_ShadowRemove in src/sysdolphin/baselib/shadow.c.
Added a C implementation of HSD_ShadowInit in src/sysdolphin/baselib/shadow.c.
Introduced an inline helper named dec_refcount that operates on a u16 ref_count pointer and preserves the special HSD_OBJ_NOREF case.

HSD_ShadowRemove now handles NULL shadow input, decrements/releases the attached camera object when appropriate, deletes shadow objects, conditionally calls func_80363DC4 when shadow->active is set, frees the texture image buffer if present, frees the image descriptor, frees the texture object, and finally frees the shadow object through shadow_alloc_data.
HSD_ShadowInit now asserts on NULL shadow using lbl_80407310, line 0xF5, and lbl_804D5F78, then initializes the texture-copy source and destination from shadow->texture->imagedesc width and height.
smart_moves: Factored the refcount decrement pattern into an inline dec_refcount helper, matching the assembly's awkward HSD_OBJ_NOREF and zero-ref behavior while keeping HSD_ShadowRemove readable.
Used explicit u16 handling and an s32 cond temporary in dec_refcount, which likely helped reproduce the original instruction sequence around refcount comparisons and boolean return values.
Kept the original redundant-looking cobj != NULL check after dec_refcount inside HSD_ShadowRemove, preserving control-flow shape from the assembly rather than over-simplifying the C.

Modeled virtual class cleanup through cobj->parent.parent.class_info->release and destroy calls, matching the indirect calls at class_info offsets rather than replacing them with higher-level guessed APIs.
Preserved direct external references to assertion string labels lbl_80407310 and lbl_804D5F78 instead of inventing more descriptive names without evidence.
decomp_lessons: For baselib object teardown, matching may require preserving exact refcount semantics, including sentinel values such as HSD_OBJ_NOREF.
Do not remove apparently redundant NULL checks if they correspond to original branch structure and help matching.
When converting assembly with virtual dispatch, express calls through class_info function pointers if that is what the assembly does.

Resource cleanup order matters: this implementation deletes shadow objects, handles active texture state, frees image memory, frees the image descriptor, frees the texture object, then frees the owning shadow allocation.
For assert reconstruction, use the original string labels and line numbers when named source strings are not yet available.
GX texture-copy setup can often be reconstructed directly from image descriptor width and height fields when assembly passes those values to GXSetTexCopySrc and GXSetTexCopyDst.
naming_conventions: Matched exported HSD symbols keep their original CamelCase-style baselib names: HSD_ShadowRemove and HSD_ShadowInit.
Local variables use descriptive lowercase baselib object names such as cobj, tobj, imagedesc, ref_count, and shadow.
The helper function dec_refcount uses snake_case, consistent with a small local utility rather than an exported HSD API.
Unidentified labels remain as lbl_80407310 and lbl_804D5F78, avoiding speculative renaming.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used an inline BOOL helper to reproduce a compact refcount decrement/check instruction sequence.
Used u16 casts around HSD_OBJ_NOREF and ref_count operations to match halfword loads/stores in the original assembly.
Used a temporary s32 cond for the HSD_OBJ_NOREF comparison, likely to influence boolean materialization and branch shape.
Preserved the function-pointer dispatch through class_info->release and class_info->destroy to correspond to indirect blrl calls.
Matched GXSetTexCopySrc arguments as x=0, y=0, width=imagedesc->width, height=imagedesc->height.
Matched GXSetTexCopyDst arguments as width, height, format 0x20, mipmap flag 0.
Represented the original assert call with extern char label declarations and __assert(lbl_80407310, 0xF5, lbl_804D5F78).
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename lbl_80407310 or lbl_804D5F78 to source-level assertion strings.
Search for other baselib decomp PRs that reuse or refine dec_refcount-style handling for HSD_OBJ_NOREF.
Compare HSD_ShadowRemove cleanup order against later changes to HSD_TObj, HSD_ImageDesc, and HSD_CObj lifecycle code.
Search for func_80363DC4 identification in later PRs to clarify the active texture cleanup behavior.
